content:
# elizaos/cloud Daily Update (Apr 28, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 28, 2026, focused on infrastructure modernization and authentication stability. The team successfully migrated the AI provider architecture from Vercel AI Gateway to OpenRouter and resolved a persistent CLI login hang issue.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**AI Provider Infrastructure Migration**
*   Completed a comprehensive refactor to remove the Vercel AI Gateway dependency.
*   Integrated OpenRouter as the primary AI provider for non-Groq traffic, with OpenAI and Anthropic direct providers configured as failover targets ([#482](https://github.com/elizaos/cloud/pull/482)).

**Authentication and Session Management**
*   Resolved a bug causing the `/auth/cli-login` page to hang indefinitely when stale Steward or Privy tokens were present in local storage.
*   Implemented auto-clear functionality for stale tokens to ensure smoother authentication flows ([#480](https://github.com/elizaos/cloud/pull/480)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#483](https://github.com/elizaos/cloud/pull/483): Push develop to production.
*   [#485](https://github.com/elizaos/cloud/pull/485): Shaw/refactor.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   *None.*

## NEW ISSUES
*   *None.*

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**Dependency Dashboard ([#79](https://github.com/elizaos/cloud/issues/79))**
*   The team is managing a stack of PRs to address dependency updates, including major version bumps for TypeScript (v6), Node.js (v24), and Rimraf (v6).
*   Progress is currently gated by a required fix for `@elizaos/plugin-rolodex` ([#7146](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7146)) to resolve `bun install` failures on the `develop` branch.
*   Developers noted that CI workflows require updates, as current configurations are not correctly invoking `bun install` or `tsc`, leading to missed typecheck failures.
